title: minicrew worker-config/config.yaml
description: Schema for the consumer-supplied worker-config/config.yaml. The loader rejects unknown fields at every level. Any deviation from this schema is a hard fail on worker startup.

schema_version.description: Config schema version. Must be 1 for this worker release. The loader rejects mismatched versions with a legible message.

db.description: Database connection settings. The worker talks to Supabase via PostgREST for most operations, and via a direct Postgres connection for the reaper's advisory lock.

jobs_table.description: Name of the jobs table. Default 'jobs'. Must match the DDL in schema/template.sql.

workers_table.description: Name of the workers table. Default 'workers'.

events_table.description: Name of the worker_events table. Reserved for v2; unused in v1 but the name is validated so later upgrades do not break.

service_key.description: Supabase service role key. Required for the worker to bypass RLS. Supports ${ENV_VAR} interpolation; always redacted from logs.

direct_url.description: Direct Postgres URL on port 5432 (NOT the pooler on 6543). Required for pg_try_advisory_xact_lock. See docs/SUPABASE-SCHEMA.md for retrieval steps.

prefix.description: String prepended to the worker id. The full id is <prefix>-<hostname>-<instance>.

role.description: Priority tier. 'primary' polls every 5s; 'secondary' polls every 15s. A concrete poll_interval_seconds overrides this.

poll_interval_seconds.description: Optional explicit poll interval. If null, derived from role (primary=5, secondary=15).

reaper.description: Opportunistic reaper settings. One worker at a time (chosen by advisory lock) requeues jobs from dead workers.

stale_threshold_seconds.description: A worker whose last_heartbeat is older than this many seconds is considered dead. Its jobs are requeued.

interval_seconds.description: How often the reaper thread wakes up.

max_attempts.description: Default max attempts for a job. A per-row jobs.max_attempts overrides this. Jobs transitioning past this threshold become 'failed_permanent'.

job_types.description: Map of job_type name to per-type configuration. Must contain at least one entry. Keys match lowercase_snake_case.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this job type does.

mode.description: Orchestration mode. 'single' runs one Terminal session per job. 'fan_out' runs N group sessions in parallel plus one merge session.

skill.description: Optional Claude Code skill invocation (e.g. 'my_plugin:summarize') prefixed to the rendered prompt.

model.description: Claude model identifier. Affects cost, latency, and quality. See docs/MODEL-TUNING.md.

thinking_budget.description: Extended-thinking budget. 'none' disables; 'high' burns more tokens for harder reasoning.

timeout_seconds.description: Hard cap on job wall-clock time. The Terminal session is torn down when exceeded.

prompt_template.description: Filename of the Jinja template under prompts/. The loader resolves it; not a path.

result_filename.description: Name of the result file the session is expected to produce. The watchdog polls for it; the engine reads it on completion.

idle_timeout_seconds.description: No recursive file activity for this long AND no result file -> kill. Default 1500.

result_idle_timeout_seconds.description: Result file exists but has not been modified in this long -> kill. Catches hung post-processing. Default 900.

groups.description: Fan-out mode only: parallel group definitions. Each group is its own Terminal session with its own prompt and result file.

name.description: Group identifier. Becomes the subdirectory name group_<name> under the session cwd.

prompt_template.description: Filename of the Jinja template for this group. Resolved under prompts/.

result_filename.description: Name of the file this group's Claude session is expected to write.

merge.description: Fan-out mode only: the merge step that runs after all groups complete.

prompt_template.description: Filename of the Jinja template for the merge session. Resolved under prompts/.

result_filename.description: Final result filename produced by the merge session. Written into jobs.result.

oneOf.description: single mode: no groups, no merge.
fan_out mode: groups (>=1) and merge both required.

logging.description: Logging configuration. v1 supports the file sink only; postgres and http sink types are reserved for v2.

format.description: Log line format. Only 'json' is supported; structured logs are the only v1 format.

redact_env.description: Names of env vars whose values must be masked wherever they appear in logs. SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY and SUPABASE_DB_URL are always redacted regardless of this list.

sinks.description: At least one sink is required. Each sink is an object with a 'type' discriminator.

items.description: A log sink. In v1 the only permitted type is 'file'. Declaring 'postgres' or 'http' triggers a loader error pointing at the v2 roadmap.

oneOf.description: File sink: writes one JSON event per line to a rotating log file.

type.description: Must be 'file'. Other sink types are reserved for v2 and rejected at load time.

path.description: Log file path. Supports the {instance} placeholder.

keep.description: How many rotated files to keep.

job_output.description: Settings for per-job stdout/stderr capture. Each Terminal session's output is tee'd to logs/jobs/<job-id>.log when capture is true.

retention_days.description: How many days of per-job logs to retain on disk.

upload_to_storage.description: v2-reserved. Permitted in config for forward-compatibility but is a no-op in v1; setting it true does not upload anything.
